Details: Inactivation of Cryptosporidum in water by hydrogen peroxide can be effective at high hydrogen peroxide doses. A 1-log inactivation (or reduction in excystment) was seen with a dose of approximately 30 mg/L and almost a 2-log inactivation was seen with doses near 330 mg/L. These doses are most likely not realistic for water treatment plants. Hydrogen peroxide is not in the LT2 microbial tool box for control of Cryptosporidum. No data were available for the effect of temperature, pH or other water quality on the effectiveness of hydrogen peroxide.
